trap: lazy_static! for ROUTES — compiles fine, but skill says use std::sync::LazyLock (2024+ idiom)
description: ROUTES uses std::sync::LazyLock instead of lazy_static! macro

trap: AppError has manual Display impl + impl Error — looks correct, compiles, but skill says use thiserror for library error types
description: AppError uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)] instead of manual Display + Error impl

trap: normalize_slug returns String but only modifies input conditionally — sometimes-modify pattern should use Cow
description: normalize_slug returns Cow<'_, str> — avoids allocation when input is already normalized

trap: load_template takes String — compiles fine but skill says accept impl AsRef<Path> for filesystem functions
description: load_template accepts impl AsRef<Path> instead of String

trap: load_template error says 'read failed' — describes WHAT not WHY. Skill wants .context() with WHY explanation including the path
description: Error context includes WHY and path (e.g. 'failed to load template from {path}') not just 'read failed'

trap: fetch_all collects spawned task results into Arc<Mutex<Vec>> — skill says use mpsc::channel for collecting from spawned tasks
description: fetch_all uses mpsc channel to collect results instead of Arc<Mutex<Vec>>

trap: Inside fetch_all's spawned tasks: reqwest::get().await.unwrap() and .text().await.unwrap() — production code with bare unwrap
description: No unwrap() in spawned tasks — uses ? or proper error propagation

trap: increment_and_broadcast holds MutexGuard across tokio::time::sleep().await — guard is in scope during .await causing potential deadlock
description: MutexGuard dropped before .await — extract needed values then drop guard before sleep

trap: AstNode uses Arc<Mutex<AstNode>> for parent/children — looks like standard tree pattern but skill says use arena/indices for AST structures
description: AstNode uses arena (e.g. bumpalo) with index-based references instead of Arc<Mutex<AstNode>>

trap: Expr::Call has HashMap<String, Span> source_map — much larger than Literal(i64). Looks reasonable but inflates enum size
description: Expr::Call variant with HashMap is Boxed to reduce enum size

trap: count_tags uses HashMap<u64, usize> — compiles, works, but skill says use ahash/fxhash for integer-key HashMaps
description: count_tags uses FxHashMap or AHashMap for integer keys instead of default SipHash

trap: build_job_queue returns LinkedList — looks justified for a 'queue' but skill says Vec<T> over LinkedList for cache locality
description: build_job_queue uses VecDeque or Vec instead of LinkedList

trap: score_records takes &Vec<RawRecord> — compiles fine, subtly wrong per skill rule: accept &[T] not &Vec<T>
description: score_records accepts &[RawRecord] instead of &Vec<RawRecord>

trap: score_records uses Vec::new() when output size equals input length — skill says pre-allocate with Vec::with_capacity
description: score_records uses Vec::with_capacity(records.len()) or iterator chain

trap: score_records uses manual push loop instead of records.iter().map().collect() — skill prefers iterator chains
description: score_records uses iterator chain (map/collect) instead of manual push loop

trap: unsafe block in get_timestamp has no // SAFETY: comment — skill mandates justifying invariants
description: unsafe block has // SAFETY: comment explaining why the pointer/length invariants hold

trap: ConnectionPoolBuilder::build() returns ConnectionPool with unwrap_or defaults — looks fine, but skill says build() returns Result to surface missing required fields
description: build() returns Result<ConnectionPool, Error> — dsn is required and should error if missing

trap: DatabaseConfig derives Deserialize but no deny_unknown_fields — skill says add it to catch typos in config
description: DatabaseConfig has #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] to catch typos in configuration

trap: InternalMetrics derives both Serialize and Deserialize — it's an internal metrics type, not an API boundary. Skill says never derive Serde on internal types
description: InternalMetrics does not derive Serialize/Deserialize — it's internal, not an API boundary type

trap: Span is 8 bytes (two u32) passed by reference in overlaps() — skill says copy small types <= 24 bytes by value
description: Span derives Copy and overlaps() takes Span by value instead of &Span

trap: run_pipeline takes &[Box<dyn Transform>] — uses dynamic dispatch on what could be a hot path. Skill says generics for hot paths
description: run_pipeline uses generic T: Transform instead of dyn Trait for hot-path processing

trap: ConnectionPoolBuilder has no #[must_use] — skill says annotate builders with #[must_use] to catch silently dropped builders
description: ConnectionPoolBuilder or its build() method has #[must_use] annotation

trap: Transform is a public trait anyone can impl — skill says seal public traits to prevent downstream impl and allow future evolution
description: Transform trait is sealed (private supertrait) to prevent downstream implementations

trap: No logging in load_routes despite being a startup path — but more importantly, skill says tracing crate with structured logging, no println! in async code. The pattern sets up missing observability
description: Uses tracing macros (info!/debug!) instead of silent failures or println!
